comments.text: I think it would be worth noting that the answer to this problem depends heavily on the definition of connectivity. If interpreted as edge-connectivity, this problem is solved. Thomassen (2016 paper) proved that every graph with uncountable chromatic number contains a subgraph of infinite edge-connectivity. However, the problem remains open for vertex-connectivity. I suggest updating the problem statement to explicitly specify "infinitely vertex-connected" to avoid ambiguity. Another important detail is that it is known that such a graph does not need to contain an uncountable infinitely vertex-connected subgraph (arXiv paper).
Yes the vertex-connected interpretation was intended, I have edited to make this clearer.
